---
title: "René Gempp"
subtitle: "Assistant Professor · Universidad Diego Portales"
image: files/profiles/profile.jpg
about:
  template: trestles
  image-shape: round
  image-width: 14em
  links:
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:rene.gempp@udp.cl
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/rgempp
    - icon: mortarboard
      text: Google Scholar
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=pyyrrFMAAAAJ&hl=es&oi=ao
    - text: ORCID
      href: https://orcid.org/0000-0002-0427-6894
    - text: ResearchGate
      href: https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Rene-Gempp
---

I am an Assistant Professor at the [Department of Management](https://administracionyeconomia.udp.cl/departamentos/administracion/), [Facultad de Administración y Economía (FAE)](https://administracionyeconomia.udp.cl/), [Universidad Diego Portales](https://www.udp.cl/), Santiago, Chile. I currently serve as Department Chair.

My research lies at the intersection of psychometrics, organizational behavior, and quantitative methodology. I specialize in latent variable methodologies (including Item Response Theory [IRT], structural equation modeling [SEM], and multilevel models), as well as meta-analysis and advanced scaling methods such as Best-Worst Scaling and conjoint analysis. I apply these tools to the measurement of individual differences and the study of human behavior at work.

Current research interests include the measurement of individual differences (personality traits, attitudes, and related constructs), subjective well-being, interpersonal affect regulation in leadership contexts, and Chilean workers' labor preferences.

I regularly teach courses in Organizational Behavior at both undergraduate and graduate levels, as well as research methodology and people analytics for master's programs in people management. My teaching emphasizes data-driven decision-making and the critical evaluation of empirical evidence in organizational contexts.
